single: The cut is parallel to the base, so the cross-section is similar to the base. Use the scale d/H from the apex, then find the marked area.
Use the labeled distance d from the apex and full height H to scale the base radius or side length. What is the area of the marked cross-section?
Because the slice is parallel to the base, its linear size is d/H of the base linear size. Find the shaded cross-section area.
First scale the base radius or side length by d/H from the apex. Then determine the marked cross-section area.
The marked slice is parallel to the base. Use similar solids with scale d/H to find the value of A for the cross-section.

global.json_output_contract: Return a JSON object with exactly two keys: "annotation" and "answer".
global.json_output_contract_answer_only: Return a JSON object with exactly one key: "answer".
global.answer_hint_number: set "answer" to the marked cross-section area rounded to one decimal place
global.answer_hint: set "answer" to the marked cross-section area rounded to one decimal place
